title: dianthus viscidus
intro: Dianthus viscidus, also known as sticky pink or sticky cheddar pink, is a flowering plant species in the family Caryophyllaceae. It has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Dianthus viscidus include its ability to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to administer its medicinal properties in a herbal preparation.
